Operation 'executeSearch' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. The original description mentions what is returned but lacks clarity on input parameters, authentication requirements, and real-time nature of the API. Added details about: (1) accepted parameters (query, location, language, pagination) to guide AI agents on how to use the operation, (2) authentication requirement (API access key) for proper setup, (3) real-time fetching behavior to set correct expectations, (4) JSON response format for clarity. Enhanced summary to be more specific about the SERP results being retrieved. These additions improve AI agent interpretability for determining when and how to invoke this operation.

data.current_description: Performs a Google search and returns structured SERP data including organic results, ads, knowledge graphs, and more
data.summary_suggestion: Execute Google search and retrieve SERP results
data.description_suggestion: Performs a real-time Google search query and returns comprehensive structured SERP (Search Engine Results Page) data. Accepts search query, location, language, and pagination parameters to customize results. Returns organic search results, paid advertisements, knowledge graphs, related searches, and other SERP components in JSON format. Requires valid API access key for authentication. Results are fetched in real-time from Google's search index.
